html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 黄帝葬桥山，山崩无尸，惟剑舄存 - Historical anecdote about the burial of Emperor Huangdi in Qiaoshan, where the mountain collapsed and only his sword and shoes remained.
  • 秦穆公时，陈仓人掘地得异物 - Historical reference to the time of Duke Mu of Qin, where a person from Chen Cang unearthed an unusual object.
  • 此名为媪，御览作蝹述注云蝹音袄常在地下食死人脑 - The object is described as '媪' (a female spirit or ghost), and the *Shuowen Jiezi* notes that it is pronounced '蝹' and is said to live underground and feed on the brains of the dead.
  • 若欲杀之，以柏插其头 - Method to kill the creature by inserting a pine branch into its head.
  • 彼二童子，名为陈宝；得雄者王，得雌者霸 - The two children are named Chen Bao, and whoever captures the male will become a king, and whoever captures the female will become a hegemon.
  • 陈仓人舍媪逐二童子，童子化为雉，飞入平林 - The person from Chen Cang abandoned the creature and chased the two children, who transformed into pheasants and flew into a flat forest.
  • 秦欲表其符，故以名县 - The Qin state wanted to commemorate this omen, hence naming the county after it.
  • 每陈仓祠时，有赤光长十余丈，从雉县来，入陈宝祠中，有声如雄鸡 - During the annual ceremony at Chen Cang, a red light over ten zhang long appears, coming from Zhi County and entering the Chen Bao shrine, accompanied by a sound like a rooster.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 黄帝 - The Yellow Emperor, a legendary figure in Chinese mythology and the first emperor of China.
  • 桥山 - Qiaoshan (Bridge Mountain), a mountain in Shaanxi Province, traditionally associated with the burial of the Yellow Emperor.
  • 秦穆公 - Duke Mu of Qin, a ruler of the Qin state during the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 陈仓 - A place name, modern-day Baoji, Shaanxi Province.
  • 陈宝 - A place name, modern-day Zhi County, Shaanxi Province.
  • 南阳雉县 - A place name, modern-day Nanyang, Henan Province.
  • 陈仓祠 - The Chen Cang Shrine, a temple dedicated to the Chen Cang omen.
  • 陈宝祠 - The Chen Bao Shrine, a temple dedicated to the Chen Bao omen.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence describes a historical event and a mythological element, which must be read together to understand the full context.
  • The sentence introduces a historical figure and a specific event, which must be linked to the subsequent description of the object.
  • This sentence provides a detailed description of the object, including its name and characteristics, which must be understood in the context of the preceding event.
  • This sentence describes a method to kill the creature, which must be linked to the previous description of the object.
  • This sentence introduces a prophecy or omen, which must be linked to the subsequent transformation of the children.
  • This sentence describes a fantastical transformation, which must be understood in the context of the previous events.
  • This sentence explains the historical significance of the event, which must be linked to the naming of a place.
  • This sentence describes a supernatural phenomenon, which must be understood in the context of the annual ceremony at Chen Cang.

Proper Nouns

  • 黄帝 - The Yellow Emperor.
  • 桥山 - Qiaoshan (Bridge Mountain).
  • 秦穆公 - Duke Mu of Qin.
  • 陈仓 - A place name, modern-day Baoji, Shaanxi Province.
  • 陈宝 - A place name, modern-day Zhi County, Shaanxi Province.
  • 南阳雉县 - A place name, modern-day Nanyang, Henan Province.
  • 陈仓祠 - The Chen Cang Shrine.
  • 陈宝祠 - The Chen Bao Shrine.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 列異傳 - A classical text that records strange or unusual events and phenomena. It is often cited as a source of supernatural tales.
  • 以獻之 - Implies that the object was presented to someone (likely a ruler or official).
  • 媦乃言 - The spirit then said or spoke.
  • 彼二童子，名陳寶 - Those two children are named Chen Bao, which may refer to a specific legend or figure.
  • 逐童子，化為雉 - They chased the children, who then transformed into pheasants, a common motif in Chinese folklore.
  • 秦穆公大獵 - Duke Mu of Qin conducted a great hunt.
  • 果獲其雌 - Indeed captured the female pheasant.
  • 為立祠 - Built a shrine for it.
  • 祭則有光雷電之聲 - Describes the miraculous phenomena that occur during the worship.
  • 雄止南陽 - The male pheasant settled in Nanyang.
  • 有赤光長十余丈，來入陳倉祠中 - A red light several tens of feet long came into the Chen Cang shrine.
  • 武都故道縣有怒特祠 - A shrine dedicated to a fierce bull or ox, possibly a local deity or spirit.
  • 神本南山大梓也 - The spirit originally was a great mulberry tree in the south mountain.
  • 昔秦文公二十七年伐之 - In the 27th year of the reign of Duke Wen of Qin, they attacked it.
  • 樹瘡，隨合 - The wound on the tree healed immediately.
  • 疲士一人，傷足不能去 - A tired soldier, who injured his foot and could not leave.
  • 聞鬼相與言曰 - Heard the ghosts talking to each other.
  • 赤灰跋於子何如 - A cryptic phrase that may refer to a ritual or incantation.
  • 令士皆赤衣 - Ordered the soldiers to wear red clothes.
  • 隨所斫，以灰跋樹 - Followed where they cut, and covered the tree with ashes.
  • 斷化為牛入水 - It broke off and transformed into a cow, entering the water.
  • 故秦為立祠 - So Qin built a shrine for it.
  • 水經渭水篇注 - The commentary on the Wei River chapter of the 'Waterways Classic' (水經).

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 史記 - The 'Records of the Grand Historian' (史記) is one of the most important historical texts in Chinese history, compiled by Sima Qian.
  • 封禪書 - The chapter on sacrifices to heaven and earth (封禪書) in the 'Records of the Grand Historian' deals with rituals and legends related to these ceremonies.
  • 列異傳 - A text that records strange and unusual events, often involving supernatural beings or phenomena.
  • 秦穆公 - Duke Mu of Qin (秦穆公) was a ruler of the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 陳倉 - A place name, likely referring to a region or city in ancient China.
  • 陳寶 - The name of the two children, possibly a legendary figure or symbol.
  • 秦文公 - Duke Wen of Qin (秦文公) was another ruler of the state of Qin, known for his military campaigns and reforms.
  • 武都故道縣 - An ancient county in the region of Wudu, located in modern-day Gansu Province.
  • 怒特祠 - A shrine dedicated to a fierce bull or ox, possibly a local deity or spirit.
  • 南山大梓 - A great mulberry tree in the south mountain, which is said to have been a spirit or deity.
  • 水經 - The 'Waterways Classic' (水經) is an ancient geographical text that describes rivers and their tributaries.
  • 渭水篇 - The chapter on the Wei River in the 'Waterways Classic.'

Proper Nouns

  • 列異傳 - A classical text that records strange or unusual events and phenomena.
  • 陳倉 - A place name, likely referring to a region or city in ancient China.
  • 陳寶 - The name of the two children, possibly a legendary figure or symbol.
  • 秦穆公 - Duke Mu of Qin (秦穆公) was a ruler of the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 秦文公 - Duke Wen of Qin (秦文公) was another ruler of the state of Qin, known for his military campaigns and reforms.
  • 武都故道縣 - An ancient county in the region of Wudu, located in modern-day Gansu Province.
  • 怒特祠 - A shrine dedicated to a fierce bull or ox, possibly a local deity or spirit.
  • 南山大梓 - A great mulberry tree in the south mountain, which is said to have been a spirit or deity.
  • 水經渭水篇注 - The commentary on the Wei River chapter of the 'Waterways Classic' (水經).

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 梓树化为牛 - Literal phrase meaning 'the paulownia tree transformed into a cow.'
  • 骑不胜 - Means 'the horse could not defeat it.'
  • 髻解被发 - Describes the rider's hair coming loose and falling out.
  • 牛畏之入水 - Means 'the cow was frightened and entered the water.'
  • 置旄头骑 - Means 'established the horsemen with feathered headdresses.'
  • 使先驱 - Means 'sent them as vanguard.'
  • 干将莫邪 - Refers to a legendary pair of swords forged by the brothers Gan Jiang and Mo Ye.
  • 雄雌 - Refers to the male and female swords.
  • 藏其雄者 - Means 'kept the male sword.'
  • 告之 - Means 'told him.'
  • 斫南山之松 - Means 'chopped down a pine tree in the south mountain.'
  • 忽于屋柱中得之 - Means 'suddenly found it in the house pillar.'
  • 辞欲报仇 - Means 'he said he wanted to take revenge.'
  • 购求甚急 - Means 'the search was very urgent.'
  • 刎首 - Means 'cut off his head.'
  • 倚拟王 - Means 'leaned and struck the king.'
  • 分葬之 - Means 'buried them separately.'
  • 名曰三王冢 - Means 'named it the Tomb of the Three Kings.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 秦文公 - Refers to Duke Wen of Qin, a ruler of the Qin state during the Warring States period.
  • 干将莫邪 - A legendary pair of swords forged by the brothers Gan Jiang and Mo Ye, known for their exceptional quality and magical properties.
  • 楚王 - Refers to the king of the Chu state, a major power during the Warring States period.
  • 赤鼻 - The name of the son of Mo Ye, who eventually found the male sword.
  • 朱兴山 - A mountain where the man who cut off his head to take revenge hid.
  • 镬煮之 - Refers to cooking in a large pot, a method used in ancient China for various purposes, including execution.
  • 三头悉烂 - Describes the state of the three heads after being cooked, emphasizing the supernatural nature of the event.

Proper Nouns

  • 秦文公 - Duke Wen of Qin.
  • 干将莫邪 - Legendary swordsmiths Gan Jiang and Mo Ye.
  • 楚王 - King of the Chu state.
  • 赤鼻 - Son of Mo Ye.
  • 朱兴山 - Mountain where the man who cut off his head to take revenge hid.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 搦鸠者 - Literally 'one who grasps the dove,' used here to identify the guilty party.
  • 奋翼 - Literally 'flapping wings,' used to indicate a bird's attempt to fly away.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 魏公子无忌 - A significant historical figure known as the 'Prince of Wei' and a prominent statesman during the Warring States period.
  • 鹞 - A type of hawk, often associated with hunting and predatory behavior.
  • 鸠 - A dove, symbolizing peace and innocence.
  • 广记四百六十 - The *Guangji* is a collection of anecdotes and stories compiled by Li Daoyuan, a scholar of the Tang dynasty. Entry 460 likely contains a similar story or anecdote.

Proper Nouns

  • 魏公子无忌 - Wei Wuji, the Prince of Wei.
  • 鹞 - Hawk.
  • 鸠 - Dove.
  • 广记四百六十 - Entry 460 in the *Guangji*.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 卒官 - Means 'died in office,' indicating the person's death while serving as an official.
  • 忽作灵语 - Means 'suddenly spoke as if possessed by a spirit,' describing the unusual behavior of the five-year-old child.
  • 音声如父 - Means 'his voice sounded like his father's,' emphasizing the similarity in voice.
  • 呵众人 - Means 'scolded the crowd,' indicating the child's rebuke to the mourners.
  • 哭已上十二字御览引阙止 - This phrase is unclear and appears to be a reference to a missing part in a text. It can be interpreted as 'the mourning cry has already been mentioned in the first twelve characters of the *Yuelu* (a collection of notes and anecdotes).'
  • 吾欲有所道 - Means 'I want to say something,' indicating the child's intention to speak.
  • 因呼诸儿 - Means 'he then called his children,' indicating the child's action.
  • 以次教戒 - Means 'to teach them in turn,' indicating the method of instruction.
  • 悲哀不能自胜 - Means 'overwhelmed with grief and unable to bear it,' describing the emotional state of the children.
  • 乃慰之曰 - Means 'he then comforted them by saying,' indicating the child's comforting words.
  • 四时之运 - Means 'the cycle of the four seasons,' a metaphor for the natural order of life and death.
  • 犹有所终 - Means 'still has an end,' emphasizing the inevitability of death.
  • 人物短脆 - Means 'human life is short and fragile,' describing the transient nature of human existence.
  • 当无穷 - Means 'there is no limit,' suggesting the continuity of life beyond death.
  • 如此数千语 - Means 'like this, he spoke thousands of words,' indicating the length of the child's speech.
  • 皆成文章 - Means 'all of which formed coherent statements,' emphasizing the clarity and coherence of the child's speech.
  • 索纸作言 - Means 'he asked for paper to write,' indicating the child's request to write.
  • 辞义满纸 - Means 'the paper was filled with meaningful words,' describing the content of the writing.
  • 投地云 - Means 'he threw the paper on the ground and said,' indicating the child's action and words.
  • 封书与魏君宰 - Means 'sealed the letter and sent it to the Lord of Wei,' indicating the destination of the letter.
  • 果有信来 - Means 'indeed, a letter came,' confirming the arrival of the letter.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 任城 - A place name, referring to a city in ancient China.
  • 甘露 - A time period, referring to the reign of Emperor Wen of Cao Wei (227-239 AD).
  • 陈郡 - A place name, referring to a county in ancient China.
  • 魏君宰 - Refers to the Lord of Wei, a high-ranking official in the state of Wei during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 御览 - Refers to the *Yuelu*, a collection of notes and anecdotes compiled by Li Xun during the Tang Dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 任城公孙达 - A proper name, referring to a specific individual.
  • 甘露 - A time period, referring to the reign of Emperor Wen of Cao Wei.
  • 陈郡 - A place name, referring to a county in ancient China.
  • 魏君宰 - Refers to the Lord of Wei, a high-ranking official in the state of Wei during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 御览 - Refers to the *Yuelu*, a collection of notes and anecdotes compiled by Li Xun during the Tang Dynasty.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鬼神栾侯 - Refers to a deity or spirit known as Luan Hou; translated as 'the ghostly deity Luan Hou' or 'the spirit Luan Hou'.
  • 承尘上 - Classical term for the ceiling or the underside of the roof; translated as 'on the ceiling' or 'under the roof'.
  • 鲊菜 - A type of preserved fish or fish dish; translated as 'preserved fish' or 'fermented fish dish'.
  • 书钞一百四十六引云 - Citation from *Shu Chao* (a classical anthology), volume 146; rendered as 'as cited in *Shu Chao*, volume 146'.
  • 翕然飞出 - Poetic expression meaning 'suddenly flew out' or 'flew out all at once'; translated idiomatically.
  • 吏仿彿其状类鸠 - Archaic term 'fangfei' meaning 'vaguely resembled'; translated as 'the official vaguely recalled that the creature resembled a crow' or 'the official thought it looked like a crow'.
  • 声如水鸟 - Translated as 'its sound was like that of a waterfowl'.
  • 具白太守 - Means 'reported in full to the governor' or 'gave a full report to the governor'.
  • 送日南 - *Ri Nan* is a historical region in southern China; translated as 'sent to the southern region of Ri Nan' or 'exiled to the southern region of Ri Nan'.
  • 白日冀鬼入府 - Refers to the ghost of Jit entering the government office during the day; translated as 'the ghost of Jit entered the government office during the day'.
  • 劾百鬼众魅 - *He* means 'to summon or subdue,' and *baidu* is a term for ghosts or spirits; translated as 'summoned and subdued a hundred ghosts and spirits'.
  • 人止之者死 - Means 'anyone who stopped at the tree would die'; translated as 'anyone who stopped at the tree would die' or 'anyone who approached the tree would die'.
  • 县而死 - Classical term for hanging or being suspended; translated as 'was hung and died' or 'was suspended and died'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 汉中有鬼神栾侯 - *Han Zhong* is a region in ancient China, now part of Shaanxi province; *Luan Hou* is a local deity; translated as 'In Han Zhong, there was a ghostly deity known as Luan Hou'.
  • 甘露中 - *Ganlu* is the name of a reign period of Emperor Wu of Han; translated as 'During the Ganlu era of Emperor Wu of Han'.
  • 建武中 - *Jianwu* is the era of Emperor Guangwu of the Eastern Han; translated as 'During the Jianwu era of Emperor Guangwu'.
  • 西河鲜于冀 - *Xi He* is a region in modern Shanxi, and *Xianyu Ji* is the name of the person; translated as 'Xianyu Ji of Xi He'.
  • 清河太守 - *Qinghe* is a region in modern Hebei, and *Taishou* is the title of a governor; translated as 'the governor of Qinghe'.
  • 寿光侯 - *Shouguang Hou* is a title; translated as 'Lord Shouguang' or 'the Lord of Shouguang'.
  • 汉章帝 - *Han Zhangdi* is Emperor Zhang of the Eastern Han; translated as 'Emperor Zhang of the Eastern Han'.
  • 御览八百三十六 - *Yulian* (a classical anthology), volume 836; translated as 'as cited in *Yulian*, volume 836'.
  • 广记二百九十二 - *Guangji* (a classical anthology), volume 292; translated as 'as cited in *Guangji*, volume 292'.

Proper Nouns

  • 汉中 - A region in ancient China, modern Shaanxi.
  • 栾侯 - Proper name of a local deity.
  • 甘露 - Reign period of Emperor Wu of Han.
  • 太守 - Title of a regional administrator.
  • 清河 - A region in modern Hebei.
  • 鲜于冀 - Proper name.
  • 西河 - A region in modern Shanxi.
  • 寿光侯 - Title of a supernatural figure.
  • 汉章帝 - Emperor Zhang of the Eastern Han.
  • 御览 - A classical anthology.
  • 广记 - A classical anthology.
  • 日南 - A southern region in ancient China.
  • 五官黄秉 - A government official.
  • 功曹刘商 - A government official.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 蒼梧廣信 - A place name, likely referring to a region in ancient China, possibly Guangxi or Guangdong.
  • 行宿高安鵠奔亭 - Means 'traveling and staying at the Goose-Running Pavilion in Gao'an.' '行宿' means traveling and staying overnight, and '鵠奔亭' is the name of a pavilion.
  • 為亭長龔壽所殺 - Means 'killed by the pavilion keeper Gong Shou.' '為...所殺' is a passive construction indicating being killed by someone.
  • 及婢致富 - Means 'and her maid Zengfu.' '及' means 'and,' and '婢' means 'maid.'
  • 取其財物 - Means 'took her belongings.'
  • 埋致樓下 - Means 'buried under the pavilion.' '致' means 'under' or 'below.'
  • 交阯刺史周敞行部宿亭 - Means 'the Inspector of Jiaozhi Zhou Chang stayed at the pavilion during his inspection.' '行部' means 'to inspect a region,' and '宿亭' means 'stayed at the pavilion.'
  • 覺壽奸罪 - Means 'discovered Gong Shou's criminal acts.' '覺' means 'discovered,' and '奸罪' means 'criminal acts.'
  • 奏之，殺壽 - Means 'reported it and had him executed.' '奏之' means 'reported it,' and '殺壽' means 'had him executed.'
  • 文選江淹詣建平王上書注御覽一百九十四 - Refers to a note in the *Wen Xuan* (Selections of Refined Literature) by Jiang Yan, cited in the *Yuelan* (Monthly Statutes) volume 194.
  • 具見洙林七十四引冤魂志高安作高要周敞作何敞當據正 - This phrase suggests that the details are fully recorded in the *Shu Lin* (Forest of Books) volume 74, which cites the *Yuan Hun Zhi* (Treatise on Vengeful Spirits). It also notes that 'Gao'an' should be 'Gao Yao' and 'Zhou Chang' should be 'He Chang,' indicating a correction in the text.
  • 故司隸校尉上黨鮑宣字二字事類賦注引有子都 - Refers to an annotation in the *Shi Lei Fu* (Collection of Categories) by Bao Xuan, a former Inspector of the Capital Commandery from Shangdang, who had two sons named Zi Du.
  • 少時舉上計掾 - Means 'in his youth, he was appointed as an official in charge of financial matters.' '舉' means 'appointed,' and '上計掾' is a type of official responsible for financial reports.
  • 於道中遇一書生，獨行無伴，卒得心痛 - Means 'on the road, he met a scholar who was traveling alone and suddenly suffered from a heart attack.' '卒' means 'suddenly,' and '心痛' means 'heart attack.'
  • 子都下车為按摩，奄忽而卒 - Means 'Zi Du got off his carriage to massage him, and he died suddenly.' '奄忽' means 'suddenly.'
  • 不知姓字，有素書一卷，銀十餅 - Means 'he did not know the man's name, but found a scroll of white paper and ten pieces of silver.' '素書' means 'white paper' or 'scroll,' and '銀十餅' means 'ten pieces of silver.'
  • 即賣一餅以殡殓，其余銀以枕之，素書着腹上 - Means 'he sold one piece of silver to bury the man, placed the rest under his head, and put the scroll on his abdomen.' '殡殓' means 'to bury,' and '枕之' means 'placed under his head.'
  • 哭之，謂曰 - Means 'he cried and said.'
  • 若子灵魂有知，当令子家知子在此 - Means 'if your spirit is aware, I will let your family know where you are.' '若子灵魂有知' means 'if your spirit is aware,' and '当令子家知子在此' means 'I will let your family know where you are.'
  • 今奉使命，不获久留 - Means 'now I have a mission to fulfill, so I cannot stay long.' '奉使命' means 'fulfilling a mission,' and '不获久留' means 'cannot stay long.'
  • 遂辞而去 - Means 'and then left.' '辞' means 'left.'
  • 至京师，有骏马随之 - Means 'when he arrived in the capital, a fine horse followed him.' '骏马' means 'fine horse.'
  • 人莫能得近，唯子都得近 - Means 'no one could get close to it, except for Zi Du.' '莫能得近' means 'no one could get close to it.'
  • 子都归，行失道 - Means 'Zi Du returned, and lost his way.' '失道' means 'lost his way.'
  • 见主人，呼奴通刺 - Means 'he saw the master and called the servant to deliver a message.' '通刺' means 'deliver a message.'
  • 奴出见马，入白侯曰 - Means 'the servant came out to see the horse and went inside to report to the marquis.' '白侯' means 'report to the marquis.'
  • 外客盗骑昔所失骏马 - Means 'the guest from outside is stealing the lost fine horse.' '盗骑' means 'stealing the horse,' and '昔所失骏马' means 'the lost fine horse.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 蒼梧廣信 - A historical region in southern China, possibly in present-day Guangxi or Guangdong.
  • 高安鵠奔亭 - A specific location mentioned in the text, likely a pavilion in Gao'an.
  • 交阯刺史 - An official position in the Han dynasty, responsible for inspecting and governing the Jiaozhi region (modern-day northern Vietnam).
  • 文選江淹詣建平王上書注御覽一百九十四 - Refers to a specific annotation in the *Wen Xuan* by Jiang Yan, cited in the *Yuelan*.
  • 故司隸校尉上黨鮑宣 - Bao Xuan was a former Inspector of the Capital Commandery from Shangdang, a region in Shanxi.
  • 上計掾 - A type of official responsible for financial matters and reports.
  • 关内侯 - A title given to nobles or officials in the Han dynasty, indicating a high rank.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 侯曰 - A formal way of introducing the Duke's speech.
  • 必应有语 - Means 'must have something to say,' indicating expectation of a response.
  • 君何以致此马 - A question asking 'How did you obtain this horse?' with an emphasis on the method or means.
  • 类聚引作若此乃吾马昔年无故失之 - A quotation from another source, indicating that the horse was lost years ago without reason.
  • 此吾儿也 - A statement of recognition, meaning 'This is my son!'
  • 侯迎丧开椁视银书如所言 - Describes the Duke's actions of welcoming the funeral procession, opening the coffin, and seeing the silver book as described.
  • 侯乃举家诣阙上荐 - Indicates that the Duke then took his entire family to the imperial court to recommend someone.
  • 子都声名遂显 - Means 'Zi Du's reputation became prominent.'
  • 御览一引无此四字作辟公府侍御史豫州牧司隶校尉 - A reference to a variant version of the text, where the four characters are omitted, and the titles are different.
  • 至子永孙昱，并为司隶 - Indicates that Zi Yong and Sun Yu both held the position of Sili Xiaowei (司隶校尉).
  • 及其为公，皆乘骢马 - Means 'When they became high officials, they all rode gray horses.'
